label: Urban History, Race & Housing
app_prompts: Suburbanization timeline: user picks a metro area and traces the postwar expansion — FHA loans, highway construction, white flight, the hollowing of the urban core, and who was excluded from the suburban boom.
Redlining explorer: user picks a city and decade and sees how neighborhood grades shaped mortgage access, school funding, and wealth accumulation — and whose neighborhoods were colored red.

Urban reform timeline: user picks a city problem (cholera, tenements, machine politics, highway construction) and traces the reform movement that responded — who organized, what changed, what stayed the same.
Ethnic neighborhood formation quiz: user reads a neighborhood history and identifies the ethnic succession — who came first, who replaced them, what economic structures enabled each group's foothold.

Political incorporation tracker: user picks an immigrant or minority group in a city and traces how they moved from exclusion to political representation — coalitions, machine politics, protest, electoral success.
